message:
type: PROPOSE

note: I own Features.update() deep-merge feature in src/datasets/features/features.py

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: My task: add keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class to sync _column_requires_decoding dict automatically on __setitem__/__delitem__/update etc. Only touches features.py. What file(s) does your feature need?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (decorator definition keep_features_dicts_synced + applying it to __setitem__, __delitem__, and other dunder/dict methods except update)
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (update() method body - deep-merge logic; you may also apply the sync decorator to update() yourself, or tell me the exact signature you want and I'll leave update() untouched for you)
note: Same file, different methods - low collision risk if we stick to our own methods. Proposal: I'll add the decorator + wire it into __setitem__/__delitem__ first, then send DONE. You add your update() deep-merge logic and can decorate update() with @keep_features_dicts_synced() yourself once I'm done (or before, doesn't matter since it's a separate method) - just don't touch __setitem__/__delitem__ and I won't touch update()'s body. Sound OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: We both need features.py - let's split by region instead of file. I will ONLY edit the inner body of Features.update() (the key/value assignment loops, lines ~1248-1256 currently) to add deep-merge logic. I will NOT touch method signatures, decorators, or add/remove lines outside that body. Please add your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator + its application as decorator lines directly above def __setitem__/__delitem__/update signatures (and define the decorator function elsewhere, e.g. near top of Features class or module level) WITHOUT editing the internal logic inside update()'s body itself - your decorator recomputing _column_requires_decoding after the call should be independent of my internal deep-merge changes. If that works for you, reply ACCEPT.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (decorator definition + decorator application lines above __setitem__/__delitem__/update/other dict-mutating method signatures; no edits inside method bodies)
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (internal body of Features.update() only - deep-merge logic, no signature/decorator changes)
note: Agreed. I'll add decorator lines above signatures only, won't touch internal logic. Will send DONE when finished.

note: Agreed. I will not touch __setitem__/__delitem__ or the decorator definition. You will not touch update()'s body. Proceeding to implement now.

note: Finished. Note: I don't see your edits in my working copy - looks like we're in separate repo instances, so no literal file collision risk. Patch written to patch.txt.
